# Satya Nadella’s Post

[!\[\]()](https://www.linkedin.com/in/satyanadella?trk=public_post_feed-actor-image)

[Satya Nadella](https://www.linkedin.com/in/satyanadella?trk=public_post_feed-actor-name)

Satya Nadella is an Influencer

59m

- [Report this post](/uas/login?session_redirect=https%3A%2F%2Fwww.linkedin.com%2Fposts%2Fsatyanadella_congrats-shantanu-on-a-legendary-run-at-activity-7437966653801320448-HL0x&trk=public_post_ellipsis-menu-semaphore-sign-in-redirect&guestReportContentType=POST&_f=guest-reporting)

Congrats Shantanu, on a legendary run at [Adobe](https://www.linkedin.com/company/adobe?trk=public_post-text)! You’ve built one of the most important software companies in the world, and expanded what’s possible for creators, entrepreneurs, and brands everywhere. What has always stood out to me is the empathy you’ve brought to the creative process and the example you’ve set as a leader. Grateful for your friendship, mentorship, and for all you’ve done for Adobe and for our industry.

- [Report this comment](/uas/login?session_redirect=https%3A%2F%2Fwww.linkedin.com%2Fposts%2Fsatyanadella_congrats-shantanu-on-a-legendary-run-at-activity-7437966653801320448-HL0x&trk=public_post_comment_ellipsis-menu-semaphore-sign-in-redirect&guestReportContentType=COMMENT&_f=guest-reporting)

For those too busy (or too lazy) to read the full note, here are the 3 key takeaways from Shantanu Narayen’s message:

1️⃣ Leadership transition is coming After 18+ years as CEO, Shantanu Narayen will step down once a successor is identified, but he will remain Chair of the Board to support the next leader.

2️⃣ Massive growth under his leadership Adobe evolved from ~3,000 employees to 30,000+, and revenue grew from under $1B to more than $25B, while its technology now touches billions of users worldwide.

3️⃣ AI will define Adobe’s next era The company is positioning itself to lead the AI-driven creative economy, with new workflows, new forms of expression, and a strategy centered on empowering creators.

Big transition moment for one of the most influential companies in digital creativity.

- [Report this comment](/uas/login?session_redirect=https%3A%2F%2Fwww.linkedin.com%2Fposts%2Fsatyanadella_congrats-shantanu-on-a-legendary-run-at-activity-7437966653801320448-HL0x&trk=public_post_comment_ellipsis-menu-semaphore-sign-in-redirect&guestReportContentType=COMMENT&_f=guest-reporting)

This visualization shows something important about the future of computing infrastructure.

Each pillar represents a fragment reused across many files. Traditional storage systems physically store those fragments over and over again, multiplying disk activity, replication traffic, and energy consumption.

The Fragmental Overlap Storage System (FOSS) collapses those duplicates into a single stored fragment and replaces the rest with symbolic references.

That changes the scaling rule:

Traditional storage energy grows with data usage. FOSS energy grows with true novelty.

The tall towers in this graph represent redundancy being eliminated. Less duplicated storage means fewer disk operations, less replication traffic, and lower data center power demand.

As AI and cloud infrastructure expand globally, architectures that store novelty instead of repetition become essential for reducing energy waste.

FOSS is a Made-in-USA innovation built to push computing infrastructure toward greater efficiency and sustainability. Cecil A. Lacy Inventor of the Fragmental Overlap Storage System (FOSS) & Fragmental Network Protocol (FNP) Patent Pending: 19/264,676

A leader who expanded the business and still kept sight of the people the business exists to serve.

In tech, scale often creates distance. Distance from users and makers. Distance from consequence. Closing that distance is a leadership choice. That is how companies stay relevant without losing their center. Indeed a job well done!

A remarkable example of leadership and graceful transition. Shantanu's legacy at Adobe isn't just about the software, but the empathy and cultural foundation he built. Seeing two visionary leaders acknowledge each other’s mentorship highlights what true global stewardship looks like. Consistency and human-centric values remain the strongest pillars for any long-term success.

Always interesting to see leaders recognize each other like this. Building something at Adobe’s scale takes more than strategy, it takes the kind of leadership people actually want to follow, and the point about empathy in the creative process is what stands out most. A lot of companies talk about that, very few build it into how they operate. And for anyone reading this while thinking about their own next move, my friends at [Offered.ai](http://Offered.ai?trk=public_post_comment-text) connect candidates directly with recruiters and only get paid when they land you a job. Leadership leaves fingerprints everywhere.
